codeReview.summary: The feature cleanly addresses the rollout-safety review findings. `CompanyLifecycleStateConverter` now preserves legacy null/blank compatibility by mapping those rows to `ACTIVE`, but any nonblank unrecognized stored value resolves to `DEACTIVATED`, which satisfies the fail-closed requirement instead of reopening tenant access. The commit also adds focused converter tests, controller-level persistence checks that assert the Flyway-v2-compatible stored values `ACTIVE`/`HOLD`/`BLOCKED`, and adjacent auth-token test cleanup that removes brittle reflection-only assertions without changing runtime behavior.

summary: PASS. Tenant lifecycle persistence stays schema-compatible on the supported Flyway v2 path, corrupted lifecycle rows fail closed, and the added regression coverage directly exercises the reviewed risk areas.
